description: Raw data explorer — understand exactly what data exists, when it was collected, which metrics are covered, how values compare to reference ranges, and where the data came from. Nothing is pre-aggregated or hidden.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Total Samples" FROM samples
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT metric) AS "Measured" FROM samples
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Flagged" FROM samples WHERE flag IS NOT NULL
SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT metric) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM metrics), 0), 0) AS "Coverage %" FROM samples

SELECT
  date_trunc('month', time)::timestamptz AS time,
  COUNT(*) AS "Samples",
  COUNT(DISTINCT metric) AS "Distinct Metrics"
FROM samples
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Category Frequencies
panels.description: One row per metric category. Coverage % = metrics with at least one measurement / total metrics defined. Flag % = flagged samples / total samples.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  m.category                                     AS "Category",
  COUNT(DISTINCT m.name)                         AS "Defined",
  COUNT(DISTINCT s.metric)                       AS "With Data",
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT s.metric)
        / COUNT(DISTINCT m.name), 0)             AS "Coverage %",
  COALESCE(COUNT(s.value), 0)                    AS "Total Samples",
  COUNT(s.value) FILTER (WHERE s.flag = 'H')     AS "High (H)",
  COUNT(s.value) FILTER (WHERE s.flag = 'L')     AS "Low (L)",
  COUNT(s.value) FILTER (WHERE s.flag IS NOT NULL) AS "Flagged",
  CASE WHEN COUNT(s.value) > 0
    THEN ROUND(100.0
               * COUNT(s.value) FILTER (WHERE s.flag IS NOT NULL)
               / COUNT(s.value), 1)
    ELSE 0
  END                                            AS "Flag %"
FROM metrics m
LEFT JOIN samples s ON s.metric = m.name
GROUP BY m.category
ORDER BY COUNT(s.value) DESC NULLS LAST

panels.title: Metrics Catalog — all 108 metrics + reference ranges
Metrics Catalog — all metrics with reference ranges and data counts
panels.description: Every metric in the catalog joined to its reference range(s). Metrics with multiple ranges (e.g. sex-specific) appear on separate rows. # Samples, First/Last Seen, and Flag % come from actual data. Click a metric name to open its detail dashboard.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  m.category                                     AS "Category",
  m.display_name                                 AS "Metric",
  COALESCE(m.unit, '')                           AS "Unit",
  COALESCE(m.scale, 'linear')                    AS "Scale",
  rr.range_type                                  AS "Range Type",
  rr.ref_low                                     AS "Ref Low",
  rr.ref_high                                    AS "Ref High",
  COALESCE(rr.sex, 'any')                        AS "Sex",
  CASE
    WHEN rr.age_min IS NOT NULL AND rr.age_max IS NOT NULL
      THEN rr.age_min::text || '–' || rr.age_max::text
    WHEN rr.age_min IS NOT NULL THEN '>=' || rr.age_min::text
    WHEN rr.age_max IS NOT NULL THEN '<'  || rr.age_max::text
    ELSE 'any'
  END                                            AS "Age",
  COALESCE(stats.n, 0)                           AS "# Samples",
  stats.first_date                               AS "First Seen",
  stats.last_date                                AS "Last Seen",
  CASE WHEN stats.n > 0
    THEN ROUND(100.0 * stats.n_flagged / stats.n, 1)
  END                                            AS "Flag %",
  LEFT(COALESCE(m.description, ''), 200)         AS "Description"
FROM metrics m
LEFT JOIN reference_ranges rr
  ON rr.metric = m.name
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT metric,
    COUNT(*)                                  AS n,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE flag IS NOT NULL)  AS n_flagged,
    MIN(time::date)                           AS first_date,
    MAX(time::date)                           AS last_date
  FROM samples
  GROUP BY metric
) stats ON stats.metric = m.name
ORDER BY m.category, m.sort_order, m.display_name,
         rr.range_type NULLS LAST, rr.sex NULLS FIRST

panels.title: Per-Metric Frequencies
panels.description: Only metrics that appear in the samples table. Sorted by measurement count descending — the most-tracked metrics first. High/Low counts match the 'H'/'L' flags recorded by the lab.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  m.category                                              AS "Category",
  m.display_name                                         AS "Metric",
  COALESCE(m.unit, '')                                   AS "Unit",
  COUNT(*)                                               AS "# Measurements",
  MIN(s.time::date)                                      AS "First",
  MAX(s.time::date)                                      AS "Latest",
  (MAX(s.time::date) - MIN(s.time::date))                AS "Days Tracked",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.flag = 'H')                   AS "High (H)",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.flag = 'L')                   AS "Low (L)",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.flag IS NULL)                 AS "Normal",
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.flag IS NOT NULL)
    / COUNT(*), 1
  )                                                      AS "Flag %"
FROM samples s
JOIN metrics m ON m.name = s.metric
GROUP BY m.name, m.display_name, m.category, m.unit
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, m.category, m.display_name

panels.title: Raw Samples — every individual measurement (most recent 2 000, time-range filtered)
panels.description: One row per sample point. All columns are shown: value, unit, flag, reference range from the catalog, lab name, and source PDF filename. Use the dashboard time-range picker to focus on a period. Results are capped at 2 000 rows (most recent first).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  s.time::date                           AS "Date",
  m.category                             AS "Category",
  m.display_name                         AS "Metric",
  s.value                                AS "Value",
  COALESCE(s.value_text, '')             AS "Value Text",
  COALESCE(m.unit, '')                   AS "Unit",
  COALESCE(s.flag, '')                   AS "Flag",
  rr.ref_low                             AS "Ref Low",
  rr.ref_high                            AS "Ref High",
  COALESCE(d.lab_name, '—')         AS "Lab",
  d.filename                             AS "Source File"
FROM samples s
JOIN  metrics   m ON m.name         = s.metric
JOIN  documents d ON d.id           = s.document_id
LEFT JOIN reference_ranges rr
  ON  rr.metric     = s.metric
  AND rr.range_type = 'standard'
  AND rr.sex        IS NULL
  AND rr.age_min    IS NULL
WHERE $__timeFilter(s.time)
ORDER BY s.time DESC
LIMIT 2000

panels.title: Flag Analysis — out-of-range breakdown per metric
Flag Analysis — metrics with at least one out-of-range result
panels.description: Only metrics where at least one result carried an H or L flag. Sorted by absolute flagged count descending. Flag % = (H + L) / Total × 100.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  m.display_name                                          AS "Metric",
  m.category                                             AS "Category",
  COALESCE(m.unit, '')                                   AS "Unit",
  COUNT(*)                                               AS "Total",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.flag = 'H')                   AS "High (H)",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.flag = 'L')                   AS "Low (L)",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.flag IS NULL)                 AS "Normal",
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.flag IS NOT NULL)
    / COUNT(*), 1
  )                                                      AS "Flag %"
FROM samples s
JOIN metrics m ON m.name = s.metric
GROUP BY m.name, m.display_name, m.category, m.unit
HAVING COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.flag IS NOT NULL) > 0
ORDER BY COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE s.flag IS NOT NULL) DESC,
         "Flag %" DESC

panels.title: Source Documents
panels.description: One row per processed PDF (lab visit). Shows how many distinct metrics and total samples were extracted from each document, and how many were flagged.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  d.date                                              AS "Date",
  COALESCE(d.lab_name, '—')                     AS "Lab",
  COUNT(DISTINCT s.metric)                           AS "Unique Metrics",
  COUNT(s.value)                                     AS "Samples",
  COUNT(s.value) FILTER (WHERE s.flag IS NOT NULL)   AS "Flagged",
  d.filename                                         AS "Filename",
  d.processed_at::date                               AS "Processed"
FROM documents d
LEFT JOIN samples s ON s.document_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.id, d.date, d.lab_name, d.filename, d.processed_at
ORDER BY d.date DESC

panels.title: Medical Events
panels.description: All medical events extracted from source documents (imaging, procedures, diagnoses, medications, vaccinations, visits). Long descriptions can be read in full via the cell inspect drawer.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  e.date                                AS "Date",
  COALESCE(e.end_date::text, '—')  AS "End Date",
  e.category                            AS "Category",
  COALESCE(e.subcategory, '—')     AS "Subcategory",
  e.title                               AS "Title",
  COALESCE(e.description, '')           AS "Description"
FROM events e
ORDER BY e.date DESC

panels.title: PDF Processing Log
panels.description: Pipeline audit trail — every PDF the extractor has seen, its outcome (success / failed / duplicate), number of records extracted, and any error message.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  p.processed_at                          AS "Processed At",
  p.status                                AS "Status",
  COALESCE(p.records_extracted, 0)        AS "Records",
  p.filename                              AS "Filename",
  COALESCE(p.error_message, '')           AS "Error"
FROM pdf_processing_log p
ORDER BY p.processed_at DESC

title: Data Explorer
